PRODUCTION_INDEX.Description: Activities of establishments whose primary output relates to industrial production.
TYPE_OF_TRANSFORMATION.Description: A statistical measure that expresses the relative change in a variable in the current period compared to the index reference period.
FREQUENCY.Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every month.
SCALE.Description: In scientific notation, expressed as ten raised to the power of zero (10^0)
INDEX_TYPE.Description: Production Index is a indicator that measures changes in the volume of output in a sector, typically comprising manufacturing, mining, and utilities.
UNIT.Description: A statistical measure that expresses the relative change in a variable in the current period compared to the index reference period.
OVERLAP.Description: This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across datasets, differences can occur due to variations in sources and methods, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and timing differences in the collection, processing and publication of the indicator.
FULL_DESCRIPTION: The Production Indexes dataset presents data on industrial production, mining and quarrying, manufacturing, electricity, gas and water supply, construction, and retail sales indexes. These statistics are collected from national authorities, and when possible, from international organizations -via data-sharing agreements- that collect similar information to reduce the reporting burden on countries.
TOPIC.Description: The output of industrial establishments, including manufacturing, mining, and utilities. This is used to track industrial activity, assess productivity, and monitor economic cycles.
TOPIC_DATASET.Description: The output of industrial establishments, including manufacturing, mining, and utilities. This is used to track industrial activity, assess productivity, and monitor economic cycles.; Datasets underlying the International Financial Statistics, one of the Fund’s principal statistical compilations. Available since 1948, it has broad geographic and conceptual coverage.
KEYWORDS_DATASET: production index;  mining and quarrying;  manufacturing;  electricity;  gas and water supply;  construction;  retail sales indexes
METHODOLOGY: International Recommendations for the Index of Industrial Production 2010
METHODOLOGY.Description: A global guideline for countries to uniformly measure and report industrial output changes (Index of Industrial Production - IIP), ensuring international data comparability.
ACCESS_SHARING_LEVEL.Description: Share this data with the public without restrictions.
SECURITY_CLASSIFICATION.Description: Information that has been published in full by the Fund and has been approved to be made available inside and outside the Fund without restriction is “Public” or “Unclassified.”
